title: Myriopus andrade-limae
intro: Myriopus andrade-limae, also known as Andrade's Myriopus, is a shrubby perennial plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The health benefits of Myriopus andrade-limae include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its traditional use in treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, including compounds like myricetin, quercetin, and andradine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Myriopus andrade-limae is commonly used in traditional herbal medicine and is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its potential health benefits.
taxonomy: Myriopus andrade-limae is a species of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). It belongs to the genus Myriopus, which is a group of flowering plants native to Mexico. The exact classification of Myriopus andrade-limae within the plant kingdom is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, clade - Angiosperms, clade - Eudicots, clade - Asterids, order - Boraginales, family - Boraginaceae, genus - Myriopus, species - M. andrade-limae. This species is likely to be closely related to other species within the genus Myriopus, which share similar characteristics and geographical distributions. Further research may provide more specific information about the classification and relationships of Myriopus andrade-limae.
